title: golemic open-pr Subcommand
summary: PR creation atomically coupled with event logging. golemic open-pr --title <t> --body <b> reads context from environment, determines the current branch, creates a PR via gh pr create, parses the PR number and URL from the response, and writes a pr_opened event. If gh fails, no event is written and exit != 0.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Dev agent (LLM) running inside the dev worktree
stakeholder_intent.goal: Create a GitHub pull request and atomically record the event in the event log
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Single atomic action that couples the GitHub PR creation with the event log entry, ensuring the event log is the authoritative record. If the PR creation fails, no event is written (no partial state).
stakeholder_intent.trigger: Dev agent finishes implementation, verify_command passes, and calls golemic open-pr --title <t> --body <b>
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: A GitHub PR is created from the current branch to main, and a pr_opened event is written to the event log

in_scope: Reads GOLEMIC_RUN_ID and GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG from environment (I1.1)
Determines current branch via git branch --show-current
Creates PR via gh pr create --title <t> --body <b> --base main --head <branch> under GH_TOKEN from environment
Parses PR number and URL from gh response
Writes pr_opened event with prNumber, url, branch
If gh fails → no event written, exit != 0
If env context is missing → exit != 0 before any gh call

preconditions: GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, and GH_TOKEN are set in the environment by the runner
Dev worktree exists with a committed branch pushed to origin

business_rules.rule: The current branch is determined by git branch --show-current (must not be detached HEAD)
business_rules.outcome: Branch name is used as --head for gh pr create

business_rules.rule: The PR is created with base main and head <branch>
If gh pr create fails (non-zero exit), no pr_opened event is written
business_rules.applies_when: gh pr create exits with non-zero code
business_rules.outcome: Error message includes gh stderr, exit code != 0, no event written

business_rules.rule: Missing GOLEMIC_RUN_ID or GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG → fail before any gh call
business_rules.outcome: Clear error listing the missing variable(s), exit code != 0, no event written, no gh call

business_rules.rule: The gh pr create command runs under the GH_TOKEN from the environment (set by the runner to the dev token)
business_rules.outcome: GH_TOKEN is inherited from the process environment; no explicit token handling needed

then.result: Error: missing env var(s), exit != 0, no gh call
Error: gh failure, exit != 0, no event

outputs.body: pr_opened event written to log; stdout may contain PR URL

errors.condition: git branch --show-current fails or returns empty
PR number/URL not parseable from gh output
errors.message: Failed to parse PR number/URL from gh output

integration_contracts.transport: gh pr create via os/exec (inherits GH_TOKEN from environment)
integration_contracts.idempotency: Not idempotent; each call creates a new PR
integration_contracts.failure_mapping: Non-zero exit → GH_PR_CREATE_FAILED with gh stderr

state_changes.precondition: Branch golemic/issue-<N> is pushed to origin

changes.value_source: pr_opened event with prNumber, url, branch

security.authentication: GH_TOKEN from environment (dev token); inherited from parent process
authorization_rules: The dev token must have write access to create PRs in the host repo
audit_requirements: pr_opened event records PR number, URL, and branch

architecture_constraints: Must validate env context before any gh call
Must NOT write event if gh fails (atomicity)
Must use the existing subcommand dispatch in main.go
allowed_changes: Add open-pr subcommand handler to main.go
forbidden_changes: Do not write event on gh failure
Do not skip env var validation
dependencies: Standard library: os/exec, os, flag, fmt, strings
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Single-threaded CLI subcommand; no concurrency concerns

acceptance_scenarios.title: Successful open-pr writes event with correct fields
given: Env vars GOLEMIC_RUN_ID, GOLEMIC_EVENT_LOG, GH_TOKEN are set
A fake gh that returns a valid PR URL
then: Exactly one pr_opened event is written to the event log
The event payload contains prNumber, url, and branch

acceptance_scenarios.title: gh failure results in no event and non-zero exit
given: A fake gh that returns non-zero exit
then: No event is written to the event log

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing env vars cause non-zero exit before any gh call

test_cases: Successful open-pr writes event with prNumber, url, branch
gh failure returns error and no event
Missing env var returns error before any gh call
PR number and URL parsed from gh output
non_functional_requirements: Event is only written after gh confirms success
definition_of_done: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test -count=1 ./... is green
open-pr subcommand is wired into main.go dispatch

decision_log.topic: Atomicity of PR creation and event logging
decision_log.decision: pr_opened event is written only after gh pr create succeeds; if gh fails, no event is written
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Schlägt gh fehl → kein Event, Exit ≠ 0'

decision_log.decision: Use git branch --show-current to determine the current branch
decision_log.rationale: Specified in backlog: 'Branch aus dem aktuellen Worktree (git branch --show-current)'

decision_log.decision: open-pr subcommand uses the existing dispatch in cmd/golemic/main.go
decision_log.rationale: main.go already lists open-pr in knownCommands

decision_log.decision: open-pr uses os/exec directly for gh pr create (inherits environment from parent process)
decision_log.rationale: osExecutor in main.go provides RunWithEnv; open-pr needs GH_TOKEN from the process environment, which is inherited naturally

codebase_evidence.finding: open-pr is already listed as a known command with description 'Open a pull request (not implemented)' and dispatches to 'not implemented' path
Production osExecutor implements the Executor interface; can be used for gh and git commands in open-pr
